If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.61-1 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/88319 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** N/A **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Pull Request QA execution **Device used:** Windows 11 / Chrome, Phone 13 / iOS 26.5 **App Component:** Other ## Action Performed: Preconditions: - Logged in to any user and navigate to any chat (I use a dm with someone that will used as the assignee test) Steps: 1. On the compose box, click "+" icon and choose "assign task" 2. Type any title then click "next" and click "confirm task" 3. Open the task that just created 4. Type "Ship it @someone@domain.com" and send it 5. Wait for several mins 6. Try with typing "Ship it to @someone@domain.com" and send it 7. Wait for several mins ## Expected Result: After typing "Ship it @someone@domain.com", the task should be assigned to that user (Assignee field filled with the user mentioned) ## Actual Result: ype "ship to @someone@domain.com" doesn't assigned to the user mentioned ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/266cfe95-a1e6-4e34-a113-b87a45798352 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

Cookies setup required for A/B tests: 1. Open an incognito window 2. Navigate to new.expensify.com (NewDot) (Very important) 3. Open console > Application > Cookies 4. Set the following cookies (check video for instructions) lc-qa = 1, lc-debug=1 5. Refresh the page Note: To bypass these conditions during QA, set the lc-qa-mocked=1 cookie. This will ignore country and free trial checks STR: 1. Sign in with an account with expired free trial and control workspace 2. Navigate to Workspace settings > add a member (Should have access to the account) 3, Navigate to Reports > Insights ## Expected Result: The banner appears on "Spend over time", "Top categories", "Top merchants", and "Top spenders" pages: "Earn up to 2% cash back on all US purchases" and CTA: "Get started" and "X" to close the banner ## Actual Result: No banner appears ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a7483ca3-8b4f-405c-9931-da5ccb44ba34 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Add Withdrawal ID to Custom Columns in Expenses Search

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Improvement
labels.description: Item broken or needs improvement.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
**Problem:** On the Expensify Card reconciliation report, you can use Withdrawal ID in both the filters and columns. However, in the Expenses reports we only allow you to filter but not set it as a column. Since this is important information for an accountant to export, we should allow them to display it. **Solution** Add Withdrawal ID to columns for the Expenses search Withdrawal ID in filters Withdrawal ID missing in columns
